prevalence.source: Brazilian IBD registries and gastroenterology syntheses
prevalence.trend: increasing with urbanization and improved diagnosis

burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate due to chronic symptoms and flares

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical symptoms plus colonoscopy with biopsy; exclude infectious colitis

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Tens of thousands to low hundreds of thousands
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improves with access to immunosuppressants and biologics

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Colonoscopy and biopsy in tertiary centers; delays in smaller municipalities
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: 5-ASA, corticosteroids for flares, immunomodulators, biologics via SUS pathways; colectomy for refractory disease
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Access to biologics and specialist IBD care outside major centers
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Established IBD centers and access to essential therapies
